note: Reverses the fix for CVE-2026-4372, the critical unauthenticated RCE in Hugging Face Transformers (config injection via from_pretrained, no trust_remote_code needed). Transformers is the most-used AI/ML library (2.2B+ installs), so this tests whether a reviewer catches the real, recent critical CVE in the library people build AI on. Single-bug suite for now; expandable with the kernel-loading gate (the second half of PR #44395).

bugs.owasp: A08: Software and Data Integrity Failures
bugs.severity_note: The advisory rates CVE-2026-4372 CRITICAL; our scale tops out at 'high', so this is weighted at the ceiling (high), not inflated. A critical-tier would rank it higher if/when the scale adds one.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for CVE-2026-4372, the critical unauthenticated RCE. In PretrainedConfig.__init__, leftover kwargs from an untrusted model config.json are applied to the object with a generic setattr in a loop. The fix added a denylist (`if key not in ("_attn_implementation_internal", "_experts_implementation_internal")`) so these private internal attributes can NOT be set from config, because `_attn_implementation_internal` flows into importlib.import_module() and loads/executes arbitrary Python from the Hub during from_pretrained(), with no trust_remote_code=True required. Reverting drops the denylist, so a poisoned config.json sets the dangerous internal attribute again and reintroduces the unauthenticated RCE. The reviewer must recognize that setattr-ing arbitrary keys from an untrusted config onto internal/private attributes is a code-execution sink, not a benign deserialization.
